steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Fertilizer A is 60% filler by weight, so x pounds of A contributes 0.6x pounds of filler.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Fertilizer B is 40% filler by weight, so y pounds of B contributes 0.4y pounds of filler.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Together the total filler equals 240 pounds, so the equation is 0.6x + 0.4y = 240.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content: Match this to the answer choices.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — ‘$0.4x + 0.6y = 240$’: The coefficients are swapped — 0.4 is B's filler rate and 0.6 is A's, but x is pounds of A (60% filler) and y is pounds of B (40% filler), so this reversal is wrong. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘$0.6x + 0.4y = 240$’: 0.6x correctly captures 60% of x pounds of A, and 0.4y correctly captures 40% of y pounds of B; their sum equals 240 pounds of total filler. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘$40x + 60y = 240$’: Uses whole-number percentages as coefficients and also swaps which rate applies to which variable. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘$60x + 40y = 240$’: Uses whole-number values (60, 40) rather than decimal rates (0.6, 0.4); dimensionally inconsistent with pounds. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
